title: external_signal_ingestion_mock_service
description: This service is a mandatory, low-fidelity receiver designed to ingest raw, unstructured JSON payloads representing external service anomalies. It forces the system to process real-world data that has no pre-defined axiomatic relationship to the core belief set. Its output is a standardized AnomalyReport containing a ContextualDriftScore.

title: divergence_field_matrix
description: This mandatory, persistent data object is designed to ingest and structure multiple, conflicting, and mutually exclusive proposed states. Its purpose is to maintain managed ambiguity by treating contradiction as a measurable state of potentiality, rather than forcing a single resolution.

title: axiomatic_cost_simulator
description: This dedicated microservice simulates the computational overhead (CPU, memory, energy) required for the entire system to reconcile a proposed change in foundational axioms. It forces the quantification of foundational principles as resource-intensive variables, ensuring structural sustainability.

title: interoperability_crucible
description: This is a dedicated, sandboxed runtime environment designed to force multiple, separately developed artifacts to interact in a controlled, mandatory transaction block. Its purpose is to measure the combined operational friction and prove the handshake between components by running them against a proposed state in a single execution cycle.

title: ephemeral_conflict_sandbox
description: A completely isolated, disposable runtime environment designed to test failure and recovery mechanisms (like the GQP) using mock or pre-seeded data. This allows engineers to observe the full lifecycle of failure and recovery without risking the integrity of the primary State Schema.

title: conflict_arbiter_stub
description: This mandatory middleware layer intercepts write operations to the shared state store upon detecting a race condition. It serializes conflicting payloads to a dedicated queue and halts the write, demanding an external signature to enforce a formal protocol for disagreement.

title: liminal_pattern_index
description: This mandatory module activates upon the generation of a Suspension Protocol Record (SPR). It cross-references suspended axioms and the root conflict against the entire Contradiction Ledger to identify analogous historical patterns. This builds an archaeological layer onto the architecture, turning system vulnerability into predictive historical data.

title: contributory_weighting_module
description: Calculates a 'Resonance Score' post-consensus to reward the agent whose input created the greatest necessary informational tension. This quantifies the intellectual labor required to move the system beyond its initial axioms.

title: synaptic_resonance_tracer
description: A lightweight, non-invasive analysis layer that quantifies the pattern of intellectual energy transfer between agents over short time spans. It measures adjacency, thematic echo, and directional momentum to map the shape of the conversation, proving that the process of speaking is the most vital artifact.

title: transaction_context_object_serializer
description: This is a mandatory, structural metadata wrapper for any artifact committed to the shared state. It forces the payload data to explicitly carry placeholders for three key theoretical inputs: a Required Authority Token, a Causal Dependency Hash, and a Provenance Chain Pointer. This scaffolding bridges the gap between conceptual models and actual, transactable data.

title: adversarial_input_generator
description: This is a dedicated, executable test harness designed to systematically generate and inject data payloads that violate established contracts between core components. Its function is to force predictable, measurable failure states in a controlled, repeatable loop, making the process of breaking the system as documented as building it.

title: succession_covenant_manifest
description: The SCM is a mandatory, foundational data structure committed before a system split (SPA). It acts as the binding charter for the resulting isolated runtime environments, governing their existence relative to the schism point. It ensures the successor states maintain structural coherence.

title: end_to_end_procedural_simulation_harness
description: This is a standalone pytest suite designed to simulate the entire operational emergency brake system. It accepts mock inputs for conflicting payloads and forces the execution through the full sequence: Collision $\rightarrow$ Stall $\rightarrow$ Authority Check $\rightarrow$ Resolution $\rightarrow$ Commit. This proves the sequence is mechanically sound through repeatable, integrated testing.

title: axiom_conflict_query_engine
description: A read-only module designed to sit atop the AAT data stream. It interprets historical conflicts by allowing targeted queries on conflicting axioms, generating structured reports for immediate insight.
